# Figure Description: Soviet-Era Geological Survey Document (Report 36572)

## Figure Type

This image contains **five separate stratigraphic correlation charts / stratigraphic columns with accompanying legend tables** — a composite of multiple sheets from a regional geological-geophysical survey. These are standard Soviet-format **"стратиграфические колонки"** (stratigraphic columns) combined with **lithostratigraphic correlation tables**, commonly used in 1:200,000-scale regional surveys. Each sheet appears to be a unified stratigraphic reference document showing rock sequences, ages, lithologies, and their lateral correlations across the survey area.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The five sheets visible correspond to different map sheet subdivisions of the M-42 and N-42 series (1:200,000 scale, northern Kazakhstan). Readable sheet identifiers include:

- **Лист М-42-II (III?)** — top sheet (partially legible)
- **Лист М-42-79** (or similar) — second sheet
- **Лист М-42-76(?) / М-42-XXXV/XXXVI** — third sheet (the label reads something like **М-42-76/М-42-XXXIIIXXXIV** or similar)
- **Лист М-42-XV** — fourth sheet (partially legible as **М-42-XV**)
- A fifth, smaller/lower sheet — partially visible, appears to be another М-42 or N-42 subdivision

The subject matter is the **regional stratigraphy** of northern Kazakhstan, covering geological time from Precambrian/Paleozoic basement through Mesozoic and Cenozoic cover sequences relevant to the survey area.

---

## Key Content

Each of the five sheets follows an identical Soviet standard format with the following structure:

### Column Organization (left to right):
1. **Стратиграфическая шкала** (Stratigraphic scale) — columns showing Era, Period, Epoch, Age/Stage in standard Soviet stratigraphic nomenclature
2. **Мощность** (Thickness) — thickness ranges in meters for each stratigraphic unit
3. **Литологическая колонка** (Lithological column) — a graphical strip showing rock type using standard Soviet lithological symbols (hatching patterns):
   - Horizontal lines = clays/shales
   - Dots = sandstones
   - Brick pattern = limestones
   - Cross-hatching = crystalline/metamorphic basement
   - Dashed patterns = various sedimentary facies
4. **Характеристика пород** (Rock characteristics) — detailed textual descriptions of lithology, mineral composition, color, grain size, fossil content
5. **Распространение** (Distribution) — notes on geographic/areal distribution within the sheet
6. **Полезные ископаемые** (Mineral resources/useful minerals) — column specifically noting any mineral occurrences associated with each stratigraphic unit

### Stratigraphic Range Visible:
- **Верхний отдел / Нижний отдел** (Upper/Lower divisions) headings are visible
- Coverage appears to span from **Paleozoic (Devonian–Carboniferous–Permian)** basement rocks through **Mesozoic (Triassic–Jurassic–Cretaceous)** and into **Cenozoic (Paleogene–Neogene–Quaternary)** cover
- Quaternary alluvial and eolian deposits shown at top of each column
- Crystalline basement shown at base with heavy cross-hatching

### Lithological Symbols Visible:
- Dotted patterns (sandstones, coarse clastic)
- Horizontal ruled patterns (pelitic/clay-rich units)
- Irregular hatching (weathered crust, kora vyvetrivaniya)
- Massive/blank sections (massive carbonates or evaporites)
- Cross symbols (granite/granodiorite basement)

### Color:
The documents appear to be **black-and-white printed** (no color lithological coding), relying entirely on pattern symbols for lithological differentiation — consistent with working/draft copies of Soviet regional survey sheets rather than final published maps.

---

Significant readable text (transcribed as accurately as image resolution permits):

- **ЛИТОЛОГИЧЕСКАЯ КОЛОНКА** — Lithological column
- **МОЩНОСТЬ** — Thickness
- **ХАРАКТЕРИСТИКА ПОРОД** — Rock characteristics
- **РАСПРОСТРАНЕНИЕ** — Distribution
- **ПОЛЕЗНЫЕ ИСКОПАЕМЫЕ** — Mineral resources

- **КАЙНОЗОЙСКАЯ** (Cenozoic)
- **МЕЗОЗОЙСКАЯ** (Mesozoic)
- **ПАЛЕОЗОЙСКАЯ** (Paleozoic)
- **Q** (Quaternary)
- **Pg** (Paleogene)
- **N** (Neogene)
- **K** (Cretaceous — Меловая)
- **J** (Jurassic — Юрская)
- **P** (Permian — Пермская)
- **C** (Carboniferous — Каменноугольная)
- **D** (Devonian — Девонская)

**Epoch subdivisions visible:**
- **Верхний** (Upper)
- **Средний** (Middle)
- **Нижний** (Lower)

- Values ranging from single digits to several hundred meters (e.g., **10–50**, **100–300**, **200–500** m ranges visible)
